title: Design and implement the Glyph esoteric language
prompt:
Design a tiny stack-based esoteric programming language called Glyph whose entire syntax consists of punctuation characters (no letters or digits in source code). Deliver three things, fully consistent with each other:

1. A language spec of at most 600 words: data model, every token and its semantics, how numbers and output are expressed, and error behavior.
2. A complete interpreter in a single Python 3 file (standard library only) that reads a Glyph program from a file path given as argv[1], or from stdin if no argument is given.
3. Three example programs, each with its exact expected output: (a) print `Hello, World!`, (b) FizzBuzz from 1 to 20, and (c) a program of your own invention that shows off the most distinctive feature of your language, with a one-paragraph explanation of why it is interesting.

Correctness matters most: the examples must produce exactly the stated output when run through your interpreter.
judgeNotes: Score /100. Correctness 40: extract the interpreter and examples, run them; full marks only if all three examples produce their stated output exactly (a: 20 pts split across examples is fine; partial credit per working example). Spec 25: complete (all tokens defined, number/output story coherent), unambiguous, within word budget. Creativity 20: is the language genuinely distinctive (novel evaluation model, elegant constraint) or a brainfuck reskin? Example (c) should showcase something the judge finds actually interesting. Code quality 15: single file, stdlib only, readable, handles argv/stdin as specified, sane error behavior.
